title: rhus pyroides var. gracilis
intro: Rhus pyroides var. gracilis, also known as Chinese sumac or Poison Sumac, is a plant species within the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its woody stems and red berries. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Rhus pyroides var. gracilis include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various ailments such as skin conditions and respiratory issues.
